input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Inertia Switch`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Inertia Switch` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: in.10.7
            TITLE: switch function

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.10.7: Switch Function
IN.10.7 Switch Function 
The function of one or more of the Primary Master Switch IC.9.3, Cockpit Main Switch IC.9.4, 
and/or BOTS T.3.3 will be verified during the Noise Test

RULE: t.9.4.3
            TITLE: inertia switch operation:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.4.3: Inertia Switch operation:
T.9.4.3 Inertia Switch operation: 
a. Must trigger due to a longitudinal impact load which decelerates the vehicle at between 
8 g and 11 g depending on the duration of the deceleration (refer to spec sheet of the 
Sensata device) 
b. Must Open the Shutdown Circuit IC.9.2.2 / EV.7.2.2 if triggered 
c. Must latch until manually reset  
d. May be reset by the driver from inside the driver's cell
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 74 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
VE - VEHICLE AND DRIVER EQUIPMENT

RULE: t.9.4.2
            TITLE: the inertia switch must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.4.2: The Inertia Switch must be:
T.9.4.2 The Inertia Switch must be: 
a. A Sensata Resettable Crash Sensor or equivalent  
b. Mechanically and rigidly attached to the vehicle 
c. Removable to test functionality

RULE: t.9.4.1
            TITLE: inertia switch requirement

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.4.1: Inertia Switch Requirement
T.9.4.1 Inertia Switch Requirement  
• (EV) Must have an Inertia Switch 
• (IC) Should have an Inertia Switch

RULE: ic.9.1.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit consists of the following components:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.1.1: The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components:
IC.9.1.1 The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components:  
a. Primary Master Switch  IC.9.3 
b. Cockpit Main Switch  IC.9.4 
c. (ETC Only) Brake System Plausibility Device (BSPD)  IC.4.8 
d. Brake Overtravel Switch (BOTS)  T.3.3 
e. Inertia Switch (if used)  T.9.4

RULE: ic.9.4.2
            TITLE: location – the cockpit main switch must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.4.2: Location – The Cockpit Main Switch must be:
IC.9.4.2 Location – The Cockpit Main Switch must be: 
a. In easy reach of the driver when in a normal driving position wearing Harness  
b. Adjacent to the Steering Wheel 
c. Unobstructed by the Steering Wheel or any other part of the vehicle

RULE: ev.7.9.3
            TITLE: the tractive system master switch (tsms) must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.9.3: The Tractive System Master Switch (TSMS) must:
EV.7.9.3 The Tractive System Master Switch (TSMS) must:  
a. Open the Shutdown Circuit in the OFF position  EV.7.2.2  
b. Be the last switch before the AIRs except for Precharge circuitry and Interlocks.   
c. Be in the center of a completely orange circular area of > 50 mm in diameter 
d. Be labeled “TS” and the symbol specified in ISO 7010-W012  (triangle with black 
lightning bolt on yellow background). 
e. Be fitted with a "lockout/tagout" capability in the OFF position  EV.11.3.1

RULE: t.1.4.3
            TITLE: all vehicle controls (steering, gear change, cockpit main switch / cockpit shutdown button)

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.3: All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button)
T.1.4.3 All Vehicle Controls (steering, gear change, Cockpit Main Switch / Cockpit Shutdown Button) 
must be operated from inside the cockpit without any part of the driver, including hands, arms 
or elbows, being outside of: 
a. The Side Impact Structure defined in F.6.4 / F.7.5 
b. Two longitudinal vertical planes parallel to the centerline of the chassis touching the 
uppermost member of the Side Impact Structure

RULE: ev.7.9.2
            TITLE: the grounded low voltage master switch (glvms) must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.9.2: The Grounded Low Voltage Master Switch (GLVMS) must:
EV.7.9.2 The Grounded Low Voltage Master Switch (GLVMS) must: 
a. Completely stop all power to the GLV System  EV.4.4 
b. Be in the center of a completely red circular area of > 50 mm in diameter 
c. Be labeled “LV”

RULE: ic.9.4.1
            TITLE: configuration - the cockpit main switch must:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.4.1: Configuration - The Cockpit Main Switch must:
IC.9.4.1 Configuration - The Cockpit Main Switch must:  
a. Be a push-pull or push-rotate emergency switch (pushing the button is the OFF position) 
b. Have a diameter of 24 mm minimum

RULE: ic.9.3.2
            TITLE: function - the primary master switch must:

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.3.2: Function - the Primary Master Switch must:
IC.9.3.2 Function - the Primary Master Switch must: 
a. Disconnect power to ALL electrical circuits, including the battery, alternator, lights, fuel 
pump(s), ignition and electrical controls.   
All battery current must flow through this switch 
b. Be direct acting, not act through a relay or logic.

RULE: ic.9.3.1
            TITLE: configuration and location - the primary master switch must meet t.9.3

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.3.1: Configuration and Location - The Primary Master Switch must meet T.9.3
IC.9.3.1 Configuration and Location - The Primary Master Switch must meet T.9.3

RULE: ic.4.8.5
            TITLE: the bspd must only be reset by cycling the primary master switch ic.9.3 off and on

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.4.8.5: The BSPD must only be reset by cycling the Primary Master Switch IC.9.3 OFF and ON
IC.4.8.5 The BSPD must only be reset by cycling the Primary Master Switch IC.9.3 OFF and ON

RULE: ic.9.4.3
            TITLE: function - the cockpit main switch may act through a relay

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.9.4.3: Function - the Cockpit Main Switch may act through a relay
IC.9.4.3 Function - the Cockpit Main Switch may act through a relay
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 89 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
EV - ELECTRIC VEHICLES

RULE: t.9.3
            TITLE: master switches

            CONTENT:
            rule T.9.3: Master Switches
T.9.3 Master Switches  
Each Master Switch ( IC.9.3 /  EV.7.9 ) must meet the following:

RULE: ic.4.8.6
            TITLE: the bspd must not reset when the cockpit main switch ic.9.4 is turned off

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.4.8.6: The BSPD must not reset when the Cockpit Main Switch IC.9.4 is turned OFF
IC.4.8.6 The BSPD must not reset when the Cockpit Main Switch IC.9.4 is turned OFF

RULE: d.12.7.2
            TITLE: the only work permitted during driver change is:

            CONTENT:
            rule D.12.7.2: The only work permitted during Driver Change is:
D.12.7.2 The only work permitted during Driver Change is: 
a. Operation of Master Switches IC.9.3, EV.7.9, Main Switch IC.9.4, or Shutdown Buttons

RULE: f.1.16
            TITLE: buckling modulus (ei)

            CONTENT:
            rule F.1.16: Buckling Modulus (EI)
F.1.16 Buckling Modulus (EI) 
Equal to E*I, where E = modulus of Elasticity, and I = area moment of inertia about the 
weakest axis.

RULE: ev.7.1.1
            TITLE: the shutdown circuit consists of the following components, connected in series:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.1.1: The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components, connected in series:
EV.7.1.1 The Shutdown Circuit consists of the following components, connected in series: 
a. Accumulator Management System (AMS)  EV.7.3 
b. Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD)  EV.7.6
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 101 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Brake System Plausibility Device (BSPD)  EV.7.7 
d. Interlocks (as required)  EV.7.8 
e. Master Switches (GLVMS, TSMS)  EV.7.9 
f. Shutdown Buttons  EV.7.10 
g. Brake Over Travel Switch (BOTS)  T.3.3 
h. Inertia Switch  T.9.4

RULE: f.3.4.1
            TITLE: minimum requirements for steel tubing

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.4.1: Minimum Requirements for Steel Tubing
F.3.4.1 Minimum Requirements for Steel Tubing 
A tube must meet all four minimum requirements for each Size specified:
